newsSince: No new June 9-23 fundamental catalyst; the ~+11% move tracks the China domestic-AI-chip theme (Cambricon's ~500k accelerator target for 2026, ByteDance as largest customer, potential Alibaba expansion). Production still hinges on SMIC capacity where top-chip yields are ~20%, a persistent constraint. Nothing name-specific changed in the window.

sources.label: Tom's Hardware - Cambricon targets 500,000 AI chips in 2026
Bloomberg - Cambricon aims to triple output to replace Nvidia

verdicts.rankRationale: Down on the CPO lens. Cambricon is an AI-compute (chip) name with little direct link to CPO/silicon-photonics/optical-connectivity bottlenecks, and after +51% YTD and an extreme valuation it is far from hidden. No fresh evidence since June 9; the +11.3% pop is momentum, reinforcing low residual CPO alpha at rank 117.
verdicts.bull: China's leading Nvidia-substitute AI accelerator; export-control tailwind; explosive revenue growth.
verdicts.neutral: Strong compute story but tangential to the CPO/connectivity thesis this page scores.
verdicts.bear: Extreme PE; not a CPO/photonics name; sanctioned-supply and single-bundle validation risk; +51% YTD discovered.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: China domestic AI-accelerator demand under export bans
verdicts.primaryRisk: Valuation; not CPO-direct; supply/sanction validation risk
